highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 ships performance improvements and adds FreeType font support for Windows Nano Server.
highlights_body: A native memory leak in the SKImage raster path is fixed, hot paths in GetEncodedText and SKRuntimeEffectUniforms are now faster, and a community contribution enables FreeType-based font rendering on Windows Nano Server.

bullets.lead: Native memory leak fixed in SKImage raster creation failure path
bullets.detail: A CoTaskMem pixel buffer allocated during SKImage.Create was not freed when the raster image creation failed, causing a native memory leak. The failure path now correctly releases the buffer.
GetEncodedText no longer allocates an intermediate string when converting null-terminated encoded text, reducing managed heap pressure on text-heavy rendering paths.

bullets.lead: SKRuntimeEffectUniforms lookup map pre-sized to avoid rehashing
bullets.detail: The uniform lookup map in SKRuntimeEffectUniforms is now pre-sized to the known uniform count at construction time, eliminating dictionary rehashing overhead when effects have many uniforms.

bullets.lead: FreeType font support for Windows Nano Server
bullets.detail: SkiaSharp now uses FreeType on Windows Nano Server, enabling font rendering in environments where DirectWrite is unavailable — such as minimal container images.

contributor_summaries.jeffska: Added FreeType-based font support for Windows Nano Server, enabling SkiaSharp to render text in minimal Windows container environments
